test: Sprint 2 T3 deployed platform BREAK: Chroma direct-access and rapid-query controls
policy_note: Kyverno/OPA CRDs were not installed in the throwaway kind cluster; deploy/policies were recorded as validated statically only for this run.
manifest_adjustments: deployment-chromadb.yaml: Chroma startup command overrides the image log-config argument so the non-root container does not need write access to /chroma/chroma.log.
deployment-chromadb.yaml: Chroma data volume remains mounted at /chroma/chroma, preserving the image filesystem required for chromadb/log_config.yml.
networkpolicy.yaml: API egress and Chroma ingress allow both 8000 target-port and 8001 service-port semantics for CNI compatibility.

checks.expected: same-namespace pod without app=eugene-api cannot reach Chroma service directly
pod in another namespace cannot reach Chroma service directly
eugene-api pod can reach Chroma through the allowed path
rapid valid /query calls with one token are rate limited after configured cap of 60/minute

actual.note: The first 60 requests reached retrieval and returned 503 because the throwaway Chroma instance had no ingested corpus; rate limiting is evaluated before retrieval and returned 429 starting at request 61.
